title: Ordering at a cha chaan teng
description: Get a seat, order milk tea and toast, ask for the bill
location: Cha chaan teng, Sham Shui Po
context: A cha chaan teng (茶餐廳) is HK's working-class diner. Fast, loud, cash-only. The waiter has heard worse Cantonese than yours. Just point and speak.
culturalFact: Cha chaan tengs were born in 1950s HK as a cheaper Western-style diner. Over 1,600 in the city today — more than Starbucks, McDonald's, and 7-Eleven combined.

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: m4 goi1, jau5 wai2 co5 mou5?
lines.english: Excuse me, is there a seat available?

lines.speaker: waiter
lines.english: Yes, come in and sit down.

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 要一杯奶茶同兩件多士。
lines.jyutping: jiu3 jat1 bui1 naai5 caa4 tung4 loeng5 gin6 do1 si2.
lines.english: I'd like a milk tea and two pieces of toast.
